get_sh_hourly.name: Get SH Hourly Data
get_sh_hourly.description: Returns a rolling buffer of completed per-hour space-heating thermal energy records. Heating Analytics calls this service to retrieve actual SH energy data from the MPC. Each record contains the ISO start timestamp of the hour and the estimated SH thermal energy delivered (kWh_th), excluding DHW-mode windows.

entry_id.name: Config Entry ID
entry_id.description: Optional. The config entry ID of the target Heat Pump MPC instance. Required only when multiple MPC instances are installed. Leave blank to target the single installed instance.

get_cop_params.name: Get COP Model Parameters
get_cop_params.description: Returns the current COP model parameters: learned Carnot efficiency (η_Carnot), defrost penalty factor (f_defrost), defrost condition thresholds, and the current LWT setpoint. Heating Analytics uses these to compute per-hour COP in the Track C midnight sync via: COP(h) = η_Carnot × COP_Carnot(T[h], LWT) × f_defrost(T[h], RH[h]).

entry_id.name: Config Entry ID
entry_id.description: Optional. The config entry ID of the target Heat Pump MPC instance. Required only when multiple MPC instances are installed. Leave blank to target the single installed instance.

user.title: System Setup
user.description: Configure the type of heat pump, refrigerant, and operation mode. These choices determine which configuration steps follow.

data_description.operation_mode: 'Full MPC' runs the cost optimizer and COP learning. 'COP monitor' disables the scheduler and only learns and reports COP — ideal for getting started or for air-to-air units without a buffer tank.
data_description.hp_type: Air-to-water (ASHP): source is outdoor air. Water-to-water (GSHP): source is a ground loop / brine circuit. Air-to-air: COP monitor only (no buffer tank).
data_description.refrigerant: Determines cold-start COP priors. The system learns the real values from your sensors — this just speeds up initial convergence. Select 'Other' if unsure.
data_description.brine_temp_sensor: GSHP only. Sensor reporting brine inlet temperature (°C). Used as the source temperature for COP calculation instead of outdoor air temperature.

data_sources.title: Data Sources
data_sources.description: Connect to your existing integrations. These sensors provide the data needed for COP learning and schedule optimization.

data_description.ha_entity_id: Optional. Any sensor from the Heating Analytics instance you want to follow. Leave blank if you only have one instance installed.
data_description.price_sensor: Optional. A Nordpool or Tibber sensor (must have raw_today/raw_tomorrow attributes). Leave blank to optimize for efficiency only without time-of-use shifting.
data_description.tank_temp_sensor: The current temperature of the water inside your buffer tank.
data_description.weather_entity: Used to fetch relative humidity forecasts, which helps predict defrost cycles and their impact on efficiency.

heat_pump.title: Heat Pump & Tank
heat_pump.description: Define the physical characteristics of your heating system. This allows the model to calculate how much heat your system can store and deliver.

data_description.heat_pump_output_kw: Rated heating capacity at full inverter load in kW (check your datasheet).
data_description.min_output_kw: Minimum thermal output at the lowest inverter speed in kW. The scheduler will test both minimum and full output to find the cheapest way to deliver heat.
data_description.min_lwt: The absolute lowest flow temperature the optimizer will consider. Also defines the zero-energy reference for the tank model.
data_description.max_lwt: The highest flow temperature the optimizer will evaluate. Can be higher than the tank ceiling if your system has a mixing/shunt valve, or lower if you want to limit the search range.
data_description.max_tank_temp: Independent safety ceiling for the buffer tank. Charging stops at this temperature regardless of LWT. Set this based on your distribution system (e.g. 45 °C for underfloor heating, 55 °C for radiators). Can be above, below, or equal to max LWT.
data_description.tank_volume_liters: Thermal storage capacity expressed as equivalent water volume (liters). For a standard buffer tank: enter the actual volume. For concrete thermal mass (e.g. heated slab): divide its heat capacity by 1.16 Wh/L·K — 30 tonnes of concrete ≈ 6000 L equivalent. For systems without hydraulic separation: enter the approximate pipe volume (minimum 10 L).
data_description.lwt_heating_cold: Target flow temperature at −10 °C outdoors (from your heating curve). Used to understand the heat transfer capabilities of your floors or radiators.
data_description.lwt_heating_mild: Target flow temperature at +10 °C outdoors. This, along with the cold setpoint, defines the slope of your heating curve.
data_description.t_room: The target indoor temperature. Used with the heating curve to calculate the minimum flow temperature needed to keep the house warm.
data_description.rated_max_elec_kw: Maximum electrical input power from the datasheet (kW). Used to detect when the compressor is running at full capacity by comparing measured power draw. Capacity learning only triggers when actual draw exceeds 90 % of this value.

learning.title: COP Learning & Calibration
learning.description:
Provide real-time measurement sensors to allow the integration to learn the true efficiency (COP) of your specific heat pump over time, replacing static factory estimates.

The electrical energy sensor is required for COP learning and SH energy tracking. The thermal power sensor (or flow sensors) is optional but greatly improves COP calibration accuracy.

data_description.electrical_energy_sensor: Cumulative energy consumption (kWh) of the heat pump. Crucial for COP learning.
data_description.thermal_power_sensor: Instantaneous thermal output (kW) from a dedicated heat meter. This is the most accurate source for thermal power.
data_description.use_flow_sensors: Enable this if you have a flow sensor and supply/return temperature sensors instead of a dedicated heat meter. The system will calculate thermal power using flow × ΔT.
data_description.flow_rate_sensor: Water volume flow sensor on the heat pump circuit.
data_description.flow_unit: The unit of measurement used by the flow rate sensor.
data_description.supply_temp_sensor: The temperature of the water leaving the heat pump (flow side).
data_description.return_temp_sensor: The temperature of the water returning to the heat pump.

schedule.title: Optimization Tuning
schedule.description: Advanced tuning parameters for the Model Predictive Control (MPC) scheduler. The defaults are well-tested for most homes.

data_description.horizon_hours: How many hours ahead the scheduler plans. 24 hours is recommended. Extend to 36–48 hours if tomorrow's electricity prices are available early.
data_description.lwt_step: The temperature increment between candidate flow temperatures evaluated by the solver. A smaller step is more precise but takes longer to calculate.
data_description.tank_standby_loss_kwh: Estimated heat lost from the buffer tank per hour (kWh) due to imperfect insulation.
data_description.start_penalty_kwh: Electrical energy equivalent (kWh) added to the scenario cost for each compressor start. Penalises fragmented short cycles and encourages fewer, longer run periods. 0.2 kWh ≈ 10 minutes of inefficient startup. Set to 0 to disable.

reconfigure.title: Edit: System Setup
reconfigure.description: Change the heat pump type, refrigerant, or operation mode. This may add or remove subsequent configuration steps.

data_description.operation_mode: 'Full MPC' runs the cost optimizer and COP learning. 'COP monitor' disables the scheduler and only learns and reports COP.
data_description.hp_type: Air-to-water (ASHP), Water-to-water (GSHP), or Air-to-air (COP monitor only).
data_description.refrigerant: Determines cold-start priors. Select 'Other' if unsure.
data_description.brine_temp_sensor: GSHP only. Brine inlet temperature sensor.

reconfigure_data_sources.title: Edit: Data Sources
reconfigure_data_sources.description: Update the core sensors and weather provider.

data_description.ha_entity_id: Optional. Any sensor from the Heating Analytics instance you want to follow. Leave blank if you only have one instance installed.
data_description.price_sensor: Optional. A Nordpool or Tibber sensor (must have raw_today/raw_tomorrow attributes). Leave blank to optimize for efficiency only without time-of-use shifting.
data_description.tank_temp_sensor: The current temperature of the water inside your buffer tank.
data_description.weather_entity: Used to fetch relative humidity forecasts, which helps predict defrost cycles and their impact on efficiency.

reconfigure_heat_pump.title: Edit: Heat Pump & Tank
reconfigure_heat_pump.description: Update the physical constraints and heating curve of your system.

data_description.heat_pump_output_kw: Rated heating capacity at full inverter load in kW (check your datasheet).
data_description.min_output_kw: Minimum thermal output at the lowest inverter speed in kW. The scheduler will test both minimum and full output to find the cheapest way to deliver heat.
data_description.min_lwt: The absolute lowest flow temperature the optimizer will consider. Also defines the zero-energy reference for the tank model.
data_description.max_lwt: The highest flow temperature the optimizer will evaluate. Can be higher than the tank ceiling if your system has a mixing/shunt valve, or lower if you want to limit the search range.
data_description.max_tank_temp: Independent safety ceiling for the buffer tank. Charging stops at this temperature regardless of LWT. Set this based on your distribution system (e.g. 45 °C for underfloor heating, 55 °C for radiators). Can be above, below, or equal to max LWT.
data_description.tank_volume_liters: Thermal storage capacity expressed as equivalent water volume (liters). For a standard buffer tank: enter the actual volume. For concrete thermal mass (e.g. heated slab): divide its heat capacity by 1.16 Wh/L·K — 30 tonnes of concrete ≈ 6000 L equivalent. For systems without hydraulic separation: enter the approximate pipe volume (minimum 10 L).
data_description.lwt_heating_cold: Target flow temperature at −10 °C outdoors (from your heating curve). Used to understand the heat transfer capabilities of your floors or radiators.
data_description.lwt_heating_mild: Target flow temperature at +10 °C outdoors. This, along with the cold setpoint, defines the slope of your heating curve.
data_description.t_room: The target indoor temperature. Used with the heating curve to calculate the minimum flow temperature needed to keep the house warm.
data_description.rated_max_elec_kw: Maximum electrical input power from the datasheet (kW). Used to detect when the compressor is running at full capacity by comparing measured power draw. Capacity learning only triggers when actual draw exceeds 90 % of this value.

reconfigure_learning.title: Edit: COP Learning & Calibration
reconfigure_learning.description: Update measurement sensors to improve COP and capacity derating models.

data_description.electrical_energy_sensor: Cumulative energy consumption (kWh) of the heat pump. Crucial for COP learning.
data_description.thermal_power_sensor: Instantaneous thermal output (kW) from a dedicated heat meter. This is the most accurate source for thermal power.
data_description.use_flow_sensors: Enable this if you have a flow sensor and supply/return temperature sensors instead of a dedicated heat meter. The system will calculate thermal power using flow × ΔT.
data_description.flow_rate_sensor: Water volume flow sensor on the heat pump circuit.
data_description.flow_unit: The unit of measurement used by the flow rate sensor.
data_description.supply_temp_sensor: The temperature of the water leaving the heat pump (flow side).
data_description.return_temp_sensor: The temperature of the water returning to the heat pump.

dhw.title: Domestic Hot Water (DHW)
dhw.description:
Optional: let the MPC schedule DHW reheating in the cheapest hours and prevent the heat pump from running DHW at expensive times.

Enable this only if your heat pump has a separate DHW tank and you can control the DHW target temperature setpoint from Home Assistant.

data_description.dhw_enabled: Enable DHW tank scheduling and COP contamination filtering.
data_description.dhw_mode: Select your DHW topology. 'Separate tank': dedicated DHW tank with mode-switching (heat pump alternates between SH and DHW). 'Coil in tank': DHW spiral inside the SH buffer tank — the heat pump always runs in SH mode and the spiral passively extracts heat at the current tank temperature.
data_description.coil_energy_sensor: Optional. Cumulative energy sensor (kWh) on the DHW spiral circuit. Used to subtract spiral load from the SH thermal energy reported to Heating Analytics, preventing it from learning spiral demand as building heat loss.
data_description.coil_daily_demand_kwh: Estimated thermal energy drawn through the DHW spiral per day (kWh). Distributed uniformly over 24 hours as additional SH-tank demand in the solver.
data_description.dhw_temp_sensor: Sensor reporting the current temperature of the DHW tank. Used to initialise the tank model and to detect when the heat pump is running in DHW mode (COP filter).
data_description.dhw_operation_sensor: Optional. A binary sensor that reads On when the heat pump is actively heating the DHW tank (e.g. a mode or status sensor from the heat pump integration). When configured, this is used as the primary DHW contamination signal for COP learning — more reliable than temperature-rise detection, especially when the tank is near its target temperature. Leave blank to use the temperature-rise fallback only.
data_description.dhw_tank_volume_liters: Volume of your DHW storage tank in litres.
data_description.dhw_min_temp: Temperature below which the DHW tank is considered depleted and reheating is mandatory. Typically 40–45 °C.
data_description.dhw_target_temp: Temperature the heat pump heats the DHW tank to. Typically 55 °C.
data_description.dhw_lwt: Fixed flow temperature used when the heat pump runs in DHW mode. Must be at least as high as the target temperature. Typically 55–60 °C.
data_description.dhw_daily_demand_kwh: Estimated thermal energy drawn from the DHW tank per day (kWh). Typical household: 2–5 kWh/day. Distributed evenly over 24 hours for planning.
data_description.dhw_ready_times: Optional. One or more times when the DHW tank must be fully heated, as comma-separated HH:MM values (24-hour clock). Example: 07:00, 18:00. The scheduler treats these as hard constraints and ensures the tank reaches ≥ 90 % of its target temperature by each listed time, choosing the cheapest hours to heat. Leave blank to rely only on the never-empty constraint.

data_description.dhw_enabled: Enable DHW tank scheduling and COP contamination filtering.
data_description.dhw_mode: Select DHW topology: separate tank or coil-in-tank (spiral in the SH buffer).
data_description.coil_energy_sensor: Optional. Cumulative energy sensor on the spiral circuit (for Track C correction).
data_description.coil_daily_demand_kwh: Estimated daily thermal energy drawn through the spiral (kWh).
data_description.dhw_temp_sensor: Sensor reporting the current temperature of the DHW tank.
data_description.dhw_operation_sensor: Optional. Binary sensor that reads On when the HP is heating DHW. Leave blank to use temperature-rise detection only.
data_description.dhw_tank_volume_liters: Volume of your DHW storage tank in litres.
data_description.dhw_min_temp: Temperature below which the DHW tank is considered depleted.
data_description.dhw_target_temp: Temperature the heat pump heats the DHW tank to.
data_description.dhw_lwt: Fixed flow temperature used when the heat pump runs in DHW mode.
data_description.dhw_daily_demand_kwh: Estimated thermal energy drawn from the DHW tank per day (kWh).
data_description.dhw_ready_times: Optional. Comma-separated HH:MM times when the tank must be ready (e.g. 07:00, 18:00). Leave blank to disable.

error.max_lwt_below_min: Maximum flow temperature must be strictly higher than the minimum.
error.lwt_mild_above_cold: Mild-weather flow temperature must be lower than the cold-weather flow temperature.
error.min_output_above_max: Minimum output must be strictly lower than the nominal full-load output.
error.dhw_target_below_min: DHW target temperature must be higher than the minimum temperature.
error.dhw_lwt_below_target: DHW leaving water temperature must be at least as high as the target temperature.
error.dhw_ready_times_invalid: Invalid format. Use comma-separated HH:MM values in 24-hour clock, e.g. 07:00, 18:00.

abort.already_configured: This integration instance is already configured.
abort.entry_not_found: The config entry could not be found. It may have been deleted while the dialog was open.
